body:
**Redundant `!modelChanged` in `else if` guard** The `!modelChanged` check inside the `else if` branch is always `true` â€” this is already guaranteed by being in the `else` of `if (modelChanged)`. The same redundancy appears in two additional locations (`src/agents/command/session-store.ts` and `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts`). While harmless, it may mislead future readers into thinking there's an independent logical reason for the explicit check. ```suggestion } else if ( patch.totalTokensEstimate === undefined && entry.totalTokensEstimate === undefined && entry.totalTokens !== undefined ) { ```
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/auto-reply/reply/session-usage.ts Line: 215-219 Comment: **Redundant `!modelChanged` in `else if` guard** The `!modelChanged` check inside the `else if` branch is always `true` â€” this is already guaranteed by being in the `else` of `if (modelChanged)`. The same redundancy appears in two additional locations (`src/agents/command/session-store.ts` and `src/cron/isolated-agent/run.ts`). While harmless, it may mislead future readers into thinking there's an independent logical reason for the explicit check. ```suggestion } else if ( patch.totalTokensEstimate === undefined && entry.totalTokensEstimate === undefined && entry.totalTokens !== undefined ) { ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Use totalTokensEstimate before input/output fallback**

When a run records `inputTokens`/`outputTokens` but no fresh prompt snapshot, `persistSessionUsageUpdate` now preserves `totalTokensEstimate` and marks `totalTokens` stale. In that state `buildStatusMessage` seeds `totalTokens` from `input+output` (`src/auto-reply/status.ts:465-466`), and this line then prefers that much smaller nonzero value over the preserved estimate. The `/status` reply will therefore under-report context usage for providers that omit `lastCallUsage`/`promptTokens`, which is exactly the case this change is trying to cover.

Useful? React with đź‘ŤÂ / đź‘Ž.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Refresh totalTokensEstimate after compaction updates**

This backfill only copies `entry.totalTokens` into `totalTokensEstimate` when the estimate is currently absent. That misses sessions which already had an estimate and then got a newer prompt size from `/compact`: `incrementCompactionCount` writes the compacted total into `totalTokens`/`totalTokensFresh` but never updates `totalTokensEstimate` (`src/auto-reply/reply/session-updates.ts:286-289`). On the next run without a fresh usage snapshot, `totalTokens` is cleared here and the old pre-compaction estimate survives, so status/session views jump back to the larger stale context size.

Useful? React with đź‘ŤÂ / đź‘Ž.
I have cleaned up the model-change detection logic across the codebase to remove redundant checks and improve readability
Updated the fallback chain to prioritize the estimate over the incremental I/O counts.
Ensured that totalTokensEstimate is refreshed immediately whenever a compaction occurs.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Skip estimate backfill when the stored total is stale**

If an existing session already has `totalTokensFresh: false`, this branch still copies `entry.totalTokens` into `totalTokensEstimate` on the next zero-usage/model-only update. Downstream consumers now render `totalTokensEstimate`, so upgraded stores can regress from `unknown` back to an arbitrary old context size even though `resolveFreshSessionTotalTokens` was explicitly hiding that value as stale. Please gate this backfill on the previous total still being fresh.

Useful? React with đź‘ŤÂ / đź‘Ž.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Prefer transcript totals over preserved estimates in gateway rows**

`buildGatewaySessionRow` now takes `totalTokensEstimate` before `resolveTranscriptUsageFallback()`. For sessions where the latest run preserved an older estimate because there was no fresh last-call snapshot, but the transcript still contains assistant usage for that run, `sessions.list` and `sessions.changed` will stay stuck on the previous context size and keep marking it stale instead of surfacing the current transcript-derived total. Before this change, the gateway row fell back to the transcript in exactly this case.

Useful? React with đź‘ŤÂ / đź‘Ž.
Added a requirement that the legacy token count must be totalTokensFresh !== false before it can be used as a baseline estimate.

If a model run was silent but the transcript reader was able to reconstruct a fresh context size from the logs, we now show that live data instead of sticking to an older estimate. This ensures the TUI and

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Refresh estimates from newer fresh totals**

This backfill now only runs when `entry.totalTokensEstimate` is absent, which misses sessions whose estimate is already populated but whose fresh prompt total was later updated through another path. Fresh evidence: `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts:366-381` persists a newer `totalTokens`/`totalTokensFresh` snapshot from transcript prompt tokens without touching `totalTokensEstimate`. On the next run with no fresh usage snapshot, this guard leaves the old estimate in place, so status/session views jump back to the stale context size. The same guard is duplicated in the zero-usage branch below.

Useful? React with đź‘ŤÂ / đź‘Ž.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Let transcript totals replace stale estimates in /status**

When a run preserves an older `totalTokensEstimate` because there was no fresh last-call snapshot, `readUsageFromSessionLog()` can still recover a fresh prompt size from the latest transcript entry. This comparison only accepts the transcript total when it is larger than the preserved estimate, so `/status` keeps reporting the older context size whenever the latest run shrank the prompt window (for example after compaction or transcript-derived prompt pruning).

Useful? React with đź‘ŤÂ / đź‘Ž.
Changed the logic to always refresh the totalTokensEstimate whenever a newer, fresh totalTokens count is found in the database.
Re-introduced the totalTokensFresh priority for transcript data. If the transcript contains a confirmed "Fresh" count, it is now allowed to override a larger (but stale) database estimate.

**Inner guard is always true after early-return** The condition `if (params.modelUsed || params.contextTokensUsed)` on line 207 is always `true` at this point. The early-return guard at line 180 already ensures at least one of the two is truthy before we reach this block â€” `!((!params.modelUsed) && (!params.contextTokensUsed))` is logically equivalent to `params.modelUsed || params.contextTokensUsed`. As written, this inner `if` is dead code. A future maintainer reading the code could incorrectly infer there's a reachable path where neither is set and the `modelProvider`/`model`/`contextTokens` fields are **not** patched, which is not the case. Consider removing the inner guard or hoisting the early-return to make the intent explicit: ```suggestion if (params.modelUsed || params.contextTokensUsed) { patch.modelProvider = providerUsed; patch.model = modelUsed; patch.contextTokens = params.contextTokensUsed ?? entry.contextTokens; patch.systemPromptReport = params.systemPromptReport ?? entry.systemPromptReport; } ``` (or remove it entirely since it is always `true` and leave a comment explaining why)
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/auto-reply/reply/session-usage.ts Line: 207-212 Comment: **Inner guard is always true after early-return** The condition `if (params.modelUsed || params.contextTokensUsed)` on line 207 is always `true` at this point. The early-return guard at line 180 already ensures at least one of the two is truthy before we reach this block â€” `!((!params.modelUsed) && (!params.contextTokensUsed))` is logically equivalent to `params.modelUsed || params.contextTokensUsed`. As written, this inner `if` is dead code. A future maintainer reading the code could incorrectly infer there's a reachable path where neither is set and the `modelProvider`/`model`/`contextTokens` fields are **not** patched, which is not the case. Consider removing the inner guard or hoisting the early-return to make the intent explicit: ```suggestion if (params.modelUsed || params.contextTokensUsed) { patch.modelProvider = providerUsed; patch.model = modelUsed; patch.contextTokens = params.contextTokensUsed ?? entry.contextTokens; patch.systemPromptReport = params.systemPromptReport ?? entry.systemPromptReport; } ``` (or remove it entirely since it is always `true` and leave a comment explaining why) How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````
Removed a redundant if guard in the zero-usage branch.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Preserve zero-token sessions in /status context line**

When the cached context size is exactly `0` â€” for example immediately after a reset/new-session flow that stores `totalTokens: 0` with `totalTokensFresh: true` â€” this `||` treats that fresh zero as missing and falls back to `undefined`. In that case `/status` prints an unknown context size even though the session is explicitly empty.

Useful? React with đź‘ŤÂ / đź‘Ž.

**`logUsage.totalTokensFresh` short-circuits the rest of the guard** `readUsageFromSessionLog` unconditionally returns `totalTokensFresh: true`, so the condition on line 482 always evaluates the first operand as `true`, making `candidate > currentBaseline || !currentBaseline` unreachable dead code. The intent is now simply "if `logUsage` exists, use the candidate" â€” but the two remaining sub-conditions create the misleading impression that there is a meaningful threshold comparison happening. Consider simplifying to a comment + direct assignment so the intent is explicit: ```suggestion if (logUsage.totalTokensFresh) { // Session transcript is authoritative â€” always prefer it over the store. totalTokens = candidate; ```
Prompt To Fix With AI `````markdown This is a comment left during a code review. Path: src/auto-reply/status.ts Line: 482-483 Comment: **`logUsage.totalTokensFresh` short-circuits the rest of the guard** `readUsageFromSessionLog` unconditionally returns `totalTokensFresh: true`, so the condition on line 482 always evaluates the first operand as `true`, making `candidate > currentBaseline || !currentBaseline` unreachable dead code. The intent is now simply "if `logUsage` exists, use the candidate" â€” but the two remaining sub-conditions create the misleading impression that there is a meaningful threshold comparison happening. Consider simplifying to a comment + direct assignment so the intent is explicit: ```suggestion if (logUsage.totalTokensFresh) { // Session transcript is authoritative â€” always prefer it over the store. totalTokens = candidate; ``` How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise. `````

Simplified the transcript merge logic as suggested by the Greptile bot. Since readUsageFromSessionLog now guarantees totalTokensFresh: true, the additional fallback checks (candidate > currentBaseline ||
      !currentBaseline) were dead code. I replaced them with a direct assignment and an explicit comment.

Updated the displayTotal calculation to use the nullish coalescing operator (??) instead of a logical OR (||). This ensures that if a session explicitly has exactly 0 tokens (e.g., right after /new),
      it correctly displays 0 instead of falsely reporting "unknown.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Preserve explicit zero-token snapshots instead of prior estimates**

If a provider sends a fresh but zero-valued context snapshot (for example `lastCallUsage: { input: 0, output: 0 }` right after `/new` or a reset), `deriveSessionTotalTokens()` returns `undefined`, so this branch falls through to the old `entry.totalTokens` backfill. That leaves the previous run's `totalTokensEstimate` in place even though the latest snapshot says the session is empty, and downstream displays that prefer the estimate (`src/auto-reply/status.ts:467`, `src/gateway/session-utils.ts:1091-1095`) will keep showing the stale prompt size instead of `0`/empty usage.

Useful? React with đź‘ŤÂ / đź‘Ž.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Don't let old estimates satisfy subagent stats polling**

`buildCompactAnnounceStatsLine()` is called immediately after a child run finishes (`src/agents/subagent-announce.ts:1488`) and this loop exists to wait for that run's usage write to land. Including `totalTokensEstimate` in `hasTokenData` makes any reused child session look "ready" as soon as it still has an estimate from the previous run, so repeated subagent completions can announce the prior run's prompt/cache and `0` I/O counters instead of the just-finished run's stats.

Useful? React with đź‘ŤÂ / đź‘Ž.
Removed totalTokensEstimate from the guard. The announcer will now correctly wait until the specific input/output or fresh total for the current run has been written.
Changed the validation to totalTokens >= 0. This correctly recognizes a fresh 0 as a valid snapshot and updates the database accordingly.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Preserve zero totals when building gateway session rows**

`resolvePositiveNumber()` drops `0`, so a session that is explicitly reset to an empty context (`src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts:330-333` stores `totalTokens: 0` with `totalTokensFresh: true`) is turned back into `null` here. That makes `sessions.list`/`sessions.changed` and TUI summaries report the context size as unknown immediately after `/new` or `/reset`, even though the store has a confirmed fresh zero-token snapshot.

Useful? React with đź‘ŤÂ / đź‘Ž.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Preserve explicit zero input/output token counts**

These `|| undefined` coercions erase legitimate one-sided usage like `input > 0, output = 0` (or the reverse). `hasNonzeroUsage()` still accepts those runs, but downstream formatters such as `src/auto-reply/status.ts:309-315` treat `undefined` as â€śunknownâ€ť, so CLI/cron session views regress from `0` to `?` for the zero side. The all-zero case is already handled by the outer branch, so storing the explicit zero is important here.

Useful? React with đź‘ŤÂ / đź‘Ž.
Changed the helper logic to resolveNonNegativeNumber() for all token fields, ensuring that an explicit 0 is passed through and displayed correctly.
Removed the || undefined coercion. Explicit zeroes reported by the model for either input or output are now correctly saved and displayed.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Preserve explicit zero-token snapshots end-to-end**

If a provider emits an explicit empty-context snapshot (for example `lastCallUsage: { input: 0, output: 0 }` or `promptTokens: 0` right after `/new` or `/reset`), this still falls through to the estimate-backfill path and keeps showing the previous prompt size. Fresh evidence: `deriveSessionTotalTokens()` in `src/agents/usage.ts:163-184` still returns `undefined` for zero-valued prompt snapshots, so this new `>= 0` branch is never reached for the reset/empty-session case.

Useful? React with đź‘ŤÂ / đź‘Ž.

**<sub><sub>![P2 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P2-yellow?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Avoid reusing stale prompt/cache totals in subagent stats**

`resolveTotalTokens()` now prefers `totalTokensEstimate` even when `totalTokensFresh` is false, and the subagent UIs render that number as the current run's `prompt/cache`. For a reused child session whose latest run only wrote input/output counters, the completion announce (`src/agents/subagent-announce.ts:554`) and `/subagents list` will now report the previous run's prompt size as if it belonged to the just-finished run instead of omitting that field.

Useful? React with đź‘ŤÂ / đź‘Ž.
